raw_91_c3: FIRST TICK = bounded sparse PPM-D order-3 with FLAT-ARRAY freq tables (PASS 1) + a26v2_build single forward sweep with FNV-1a 12-bit slot + linear probing (PASS 2) + a26v2_lookup + a26v2_estimate_bits in-sample estimator (PASS 2.5) + 5-fixture selftest + 3 memory probes byte-eq PASS (PASS 3). PASS 4 LIVE FIRE 6-repo measurement DEFERRED to subsequent tick per raw 91 honest C3. WIRE coder = identity passthrough on first tick (no fabricated saving on wire axis). Empirical memory measurement on 83KB n6-architecture/README.md: peak RSS 489MB EXCEEDS the F-A26-V2-4 200MB production gate — partial TRIP. The O(n²) map-copy blowup that drove v1 to 1.2GB on 8KB IS eliminated (8KB v2 = 67MB ≪ 1.2GB v1 = 18x reduction; 83KB v2 = 489MB ≪ KILLED v1). However absolute memory still exceeds production gate. raw 91 honest C3: do NOT promote wire coder this tick; subsequent-tick options = (a) drop max_order to 2, (b) reduce A26_V2_CTX_LIMIT to 1024, (c) inline freq array as fixed buffer to avoid hexa per-row alloc overhead, (d) recommend cross-pivot to A19/A24/A25 if production gate cannot be reached.

trend: v2 memory grows roughly linearly with input size (37 → 67 → 117 → 489 MB for 4KB → 8KB → 16KB → 83KB), confirming O(n²) elimination. v1 was super-linear (338 → 1167 MB at 4KB → 8KB, KILLED at higher). Slope is approximately 6 KB-input per MB-RSS for v2. Production gate of 200MB is reached at ~32KB input — current implementation cannot scale to full 6-repo aggregate (9.92MB) without further reduction.
